meta.name: Brief-COPE Coping Inventory
meta.description: The Brief-COPE is a 28-item self-report scale that measures 14 distinct coping strategies people use in response to stressful life events. It is a shortened version of the full COPE Inventory and is widely used in health psychology and stress research.
meta.author: Carver (1997)
meta.reference: Carver CS. You want to measure coping but your protocol is too long: consider the Brief COPE. Int J Behav Med. 1997;4(1):92-100.
meta.instruction: The following items ask what you have been doing to cope with a stressful event. Each item says something about a particular way of coping. Please indicate the extent to which you have been doing what the item says.

commonChoices.label: I haven't been doing this at all
I've been doing this a little bit
I've been doing this a medium amount
I've been doing this a lot

questions.text: I've been turning to work or other activities to take my mind off things.
I've been concentrating my efforts on doing something about the situation I'm in.
I've been saying to myself "this isn't real."
I've been using alcohol or other drugs to make myself feel better.
I've been getting emotional support from others.
I've been giving up trying to deal with it.
I've been taking action to try to make the situation better.
I've been refusing to believe that it has happened.
I've been saying things to let my unpleasant feelings escape.
I've been getting help and advice from other people.
I've been using alcohol or other drugs to help me get through it.
I've been trying to see it in a different light, to make it seem more positive.
I've been criticizing myself.
I've been trying to come up with a strategy about what to do.
I've been getting comfort and understanding from someone.
I've been giving up the attempt to cope.
I've been looking for something good in what is happening.
I've been making jokes about it.
I've been doing something to think about it less, such as going to movies, watching TV, reading, daydreaming, sleeping, or shopping.
I've been accepting the reality of the fact that it has happened.
I've been expressing my negative feelings.
I've been trying to find comfort in my religion or spiritual beliefs.
I've been trying to get advice or help from other people about what to do.
I've been learning to live with it.
I've been thinking hard about what steps to take.
I've been blaming myself for things that happened.
I've been praying or meditating.
I've been making fun of the situation.

ranges.label: Low Coping Engagement
ranges.description: You report relatively low use of coping strategies overall. This indicates that your current stress levels are manageable and you are coping well with daily challenges.
suggestions: Consider exploring different coping strategies
Even small coping efforts can make a difference
Continue using your effective stress management strategies
Regularly check in with yourself and engage in relaxation activities
Maintain a healthy work-life balance

ranges.label: Moderate Coping Engagement
ranges.description: You report moderate use of coping strategies. Review the subscale scores. At this level, stress may cause persistent tension, mood fluctuations, and reduced work efficiency or rest quality.
suggestions: Examine which coping strategies are most helpful for you
Consider whether your coping mix includes enough adaptive strategies
Consider discussing stress management strategies with a counselor
Evaluate and adjust major stressors in your work or personal life
Build daily habits of relaxation and self-care

ranges.label: High Coping Engagement
ranges.description: You report high use of coping strategies. Check the subscale breakdown. Your stress level is elevated and may be significantly affecting your physical and mental health. Seeking support and making adjustments is recommended.
suggestions: Review subscale scores to ensure you rely more on adaptive strategies
High substance use or avoidance scores may warrant professional consultation
Seek professional counseling support
Evaluate whether adjustments to your current work or life situation are needed
Learn emergency stress-relief techniques for high-pressure moments

self_distraction.label: Low
self_distraction.description: Low use of self-distraction as a coping strategy. This indicates that your current stress levels are manageable and you are coping well with daily challenges.
suggestions: Continue using your effective stress management strategies
Regularly check in with yourself and engage in relaxation activities
Maintain a healthy work-life balance
Cultivate hobbies and interests that bring you joy
Nurture healthy social relationships

self_distraction.label: Moderate
self_distraction.description: Moderate use of self-distraction as a coping strategy. At this level, stress may cause persistent tension, mood fluctuations, and reduced work efficiency or rest quality.
suggestions: Consider discussing stress management strategies with a counselor
Evaluate and adjust major stressors in your work or personal life
Build daily habits of relaxation and self-care
Consider reducing unnecessary commitments and responsibilities
Ensure adequate sleep time

self_distraction.label: High
self_distraction.description: High use of self-distraction as a coping strategy. Your stress level is elevated and may be significantly affecting your physical and mental health. Seeking support and making adjustments is recommended.
suggestions: Seek professional counseling support
Evaluate whether adjustments to your current work or life situation are needed
Learn emergency stress-relief techniques for high-pressure moments
Ensure basic needs for sleep, nutrition, and rest are met
Consider temporarily reducing workload or seeking help to share responsibilities

emotional_support.label: Low
emotional_support.description: Low use of emotional support as a coping strategy. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities
Remember to seek support when facing difficulties

emotional_support.label: Moderate
emotional_support.description: Moderate use of emotional support as a coping strategy. At this level, psychological concerns may be affecting your daily life. It is recommended to take active steps and consider available support options.
suggestions: Consider consulting a mental health professional
Identify your specific sources of concern
Maintain a structured daily routine
Try relaxation and coping methods that suit you
Engage in meaningful activities and social interactions

emotional_support.label: High
emotional_support.description: High use of emotional support as a coping strategy. Your mental health concerns are notable and may be impacting daily functioning. Speaking with a mental health professional is recommended.
suggestions: Schedule a mental health professional assessment as soon as possible
Make sure you have trusted people around you for support
Follow the treatment plan recommended by your professional
Maintain basic daily self-care routines
If you feel overwhelmed, call a mental health helpline for immediate support

emotional_support.label: Very High
emotional_support.description: Very high use of emotional support as a coping strategy. Your mental health concerns are notable and may be impacting daily functioning. Speaking with a mental health professional is recommended.
suggestions: Schedule a mental health professional assessment as soon as possible
Make sure you have trusted people around you for support
Follow the treatment plan recommended by your professional
Maintain basic daily self-care routines
If you feel overwhelmed, call a mental health helpline for immediate support

behavioral_disengagement.label: Low
behavioral_disengagement.description: Low use of behavioral disengagement as a coping strategy. This indicates that the relevant behavioral characteristics are within the normal range with no significant functional impact.
suggestions: Understand your behavioral characteristics and leverage your strengths
Maintain your current effective coping strategies
Seek information and support when needed

behavioral_disengagement.label: Moderate
behavioral_disengagement.description: Moderate use of behavioral disengagement as a coping strategy. At this level, behavioral characteristics may have some impact on learning, work, or social interactions. Understanding and adapting strategies can be helpful.
suggestions: Consider a more comprehensive professional assessment
Learn specific behavioral management strategies and skills
Communicate with your school or workplace to obtain appropriate support
Try establishing structured daily routines
Consider attending relevant skill-building courses

behavioral_disengagement.label: High
behavioral_disengagement.description: High use of behavioral disengagement as a coping strategy. Your behavioral characteristic scores are elevated and may be considerably affecting daily functioning. Professional assessment and support is recommended.
suggestions: Seek professional assessment and ongoing support
A combination of behavioral interventions and other support measures may be needed
Work with family and relevant organizations to develop a support plan
Focus on overall physical and mental well-being and quality of life

behavioral_disengagement.label: Very High

Focus on overall physical and mental well-being and quality of life
